title: Holding the bureaucracy accountable
description: Test your knowledge on how the other branches hold the bureaucracy accountable as it implements policy!

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements explains how Congress can use its power of the purse to restrict presidential power?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Congress can deny requests for funds to bureaucratic agencies during the budget process.
Congress determines the president’s salary and can cut the president’s salary based on performance.
Congress drafts and passes a federal budget that does not require presidential approval.
Congress allocates funds for presidential campaigns and can restrict funds to the president’s party.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios is an example of how Congress oversees the bureaucracy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Congress calls several high-level officials of the Department of Homeland Security to testify on cross-agency communication after September 11th.
Congress confirms the head of the Environmental Protection Agency in both the House and the Senate.
Congress overrides a presidential veto of a bill that decriminalizes the sale of cannabis with a two-thirds veto.
Congress gives the Securities and Exchange Commission discretionary authority in a bill regulating Wall Street companies.

assessment_items.question_content:
Members of the House Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure are concerned about a rise in the number of accidents on national highways. 

**Which of the following is an action the committee can take to address the problem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: holding a hearing and subpoenaing the testimony of high-level officials of the Department of Transportation
confiscating funds from the Department of Transportation until they resolve the problems
replacing the secretary of the Department of Transportation with a member of the committee
disbanding the Department of Transportation and allocating its responsibilities to other executive departments

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios best illustrates a formal check on the federal bureaucracy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Senate Committee on the Budget proposes reducing the amount of money the Environmental Protection Agency gets for the 2021 fiscal year when setting the annual budget.
The president nominates a former teacher for the role of Secretary of Education after the previous Secretary of Education runs for public office in her home state.
The Supreme Court rules that an executive order instructing the Secretary of Energy to seize all American coal mines and shut them down is unconstitutional.
The Department of Transportation sets new regulations detailing safety standards for national highways, requiring states to devote some of their budget to restoring these highways.

assessment_items.question_content:
President David Rose refuses to spend the money Congress appropriated for the Environmental Protection Agency. In response, Congress has rejected the [[☃ definition 1]] of funds. 

**This scenario illustrates:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: How Congress uses its oversight powers to check the executive branch’s budgeting power.
How the goals of the executive branch can influence the policy goals of the federal bureaucracy.
How the bureaucracy and Congress work together to implement important policy.
How non-governmental agencies can influence the budget setting process.

assessment_items.question_content:
President Jane Villanueva orders the Federal Election Commission to set limits on how much candidates can spend on campaign advertisements. 

**Which of the following actions could Congress take to limit the president’s authorization?**   

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Pass legislation to restrict the Federal Election Commission’s power to make rules about how candidates spend their money
Remove the commissioners of the Federal Election Commission and replace them with people who will not enforce the president’s order
Sue the Federal Election Commission and let the Supreme Court rule on the constitutionality of the president’s order
Start impeachment proceedings to remove the president from her position of power over the Federal Election Commission

assessment_items.question_content:
The Department of Education issues new regulations that expand eligibility for a student loan forgiveness program to include borrowers with private loans. Some lawmakers argue that this change exceeds the authority granted by Congress. In response, the House Committee on Education and the Workforce holds a hearing and questions the Secretary of Education about the legal basis for the new rules.

**Which method of congressional oversight is illustrated in this scenario?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Reviewing how an agency is carrying out a law
Rewriting legislation
Withholding funding
Seeking judicial intervention to enforce limits on executive power

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why might Congress launch an investigation into a federal agency’s activities?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To determine whether the agency is acting within its legal authority
To rewrite the law to reflect the agency’s preferences
To support the president’s broader administrative goals and policy agendas
To evaluate agency leaders and appoint new leadership when necessary

assessment_items.question_content:
**How does Congress use the power of the purse to check the federal bureaucracy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: By deciding how much funding agencies receive in the federal budget
By passing legislation that authorizes agency operations
By confirming presidential appointments to lead federal agencies
By passing legislation that limits how agencies interpret federal laws

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios best reflects how a president’s ideology can affect bureaucratic agencies?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The president appoints an administrator who shares her philosophy that the federal government should shrink and empower the states to implement policy.
The House Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure cut the Department of Transportation’s budget for the 2021 fiscal year after the president submits his budget.
The Environmental Protection Agency works with academics, journalists, and lobbyists to influence environmental policy and interfere with the president’s control of the agency.
The president nominates an ardent campaign supporter for an ambassador position in Switzerland as a reward for the loyalty she showed during the campaign.

assessment_items.question_content:
President Alice Pieszecki divides the Department of Education into two bureaucratic agencies: the Department of K-12 Education and the Higher Education Agency. She hopes that by separating the department into two agencies, she can get the Higher Education Agency to work on addressing student loan debt. 

**This scenario illustrates:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how the president’s reorganization authority can force bureaucratic agencies to meet their administration’s goals.
how Congress gives bureaucratic agencies discretionary authority to determine how best to implement public policy.
how the president nominates people who have relevant qualifications and experience to roles in bureaucratic agencies.
how bureaucratic agencies can work with interest groups and Congress to formulate policy.

assessment_items.question_content:
At his first State of the Union, President Jason Mendoza uses the platform to inform the American public and the other branches of government of his policy goals for the first year of his presidency. His policy goals include supporting the buffalo wing industry and making Blake Bortles the king of Florida. 

**The president’s agenda-setting power can have which of the following effects on bureaucratic agencies?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It pushes bureaucratic agencies to execute and follow through on the president’s policy goals.
It informs bureaucratic agencies about how they should spend their budget.
It gives Congress the power to reorganize bureaucratic agencies in service of the president’s policy goals.
It limits the discretionary authority of bureaucratic agencies as they have to support the president.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Compliance monitoring has which of the following effects on the bureaucracy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It challenges bureaucratic agencies’ ability to implement a policy that matches the president’s goals.
It pushes bureaucratic agencies to enforce rules and regulations at the local level, bypassing state governments.
It strengthens the relationship between the bureaucracy, Congress, and interest groups to formulate public policy.
It leads to more expertise in bureaucratic agencies as presidents appoint people with relevant qualifications and experience.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following provides the most accurate explanation of how compliance monitoring poses a challenge to policy implementation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: State and local agencies may shape policy in different directions than the federal bureaucracy intended, which forces the federal bureaucracy to spend resources enforcing compliance with the original policy.
Compliance monitoring requires that bureaucratic agencies have strong relationships with state and local agencies, dividing their loyalty and impeding their relationship with the president.
Bureaucratic agencies have power to make rules and regulations on policy, but do not have enforcement power as that power falls to state and local governments.

Local agencies have to report their compliance to federal policies to state agencies who send those reports to federal agencies, slowing the direct line of communication between federal and local agencies.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best defines compliance monitoring?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the responsibility of bureaucratic agencies to ensure that actors in their jurisdiction obey all regulations they set
the ability to make legally-binding standards that all persons under the agency’s authority must follow
the authority to decide how to implement a law and decide Congress’s intention when it passed the law
the actions Congress can take to hold bureaucratic officials accountable when implementing policy and limit bureaucratic powers
